votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq string splitting, counting, and specification logic, correctly verifying the histogram properties including maximum frequency, no duplicates, and exact matches.
The Python code correctly translates the Coq specification, including the string splitting, counting, and the conditions for the histogram result.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq string processing, counting, and specification logic, correctly verifying all conditions of the histogram specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. split_aux/split_spaces split strings by spaces identically. count_occurrences and get_max_count compute the same values. The histogram_spec function checks the same conditions: empty words implies empty result, NoDup on keys, all result entries have value mx and their count equals mx, and all words with max count appear in result with value mx. The logic matches the Coq Prop specification for concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. split_aux/split_spaces split on spaces identically. count_occurrences and get_max_count compute the same values. The histogram_spec function checks the same three conditions: (1) empty words implies empty result, (2) NoDup on keys, (3) all entries in result have value mx and their count equals mx, (4) all words with max count appear in result with value mx. The Python implementation faithfully translates these logical conditions into executable checks that produce the same boolean judgment for any concrete inputs.

The Python implementation faithfully translates these logical conditions into executable checks that will return the same boolean judgment for any concrete inputs.

Python mirrors Coq’s splitting, counting, max computation, lookup, and histogram conditions (including empty case and unique keys). The logical implications are enforced equivalently, so results match for all inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq split on spaces, counting, max, and lookup; the histogram_spec checks (empty case, NoDup on keys, all entries have count mx, and all max-occurring words are present) match the Coq Prop for all inputs.

Python split, counting, max, lookup, and spec checks mirror the Coq definitions, including handling of empty/non-empty words, uniqueness of keys, and inclusion/exclusion conditions for max-frequency words.
